transcript.clean_text: the temperature was rising at the 2023 Tour de France which is available to watch live and On Demand on gcn Plus the heat was turned off on the sprinters and the GC contenders after two days in the Pyrenees it was likely going to be down to the fast men to finish in waldor only one fast man had one so far in this tour Jasper Phillips in the green jersey and looking good the sage would leave mondumasso and head to Bordeaux the start town have been the longtime residents of Luis ocano when he was racing after leaving Spain this one much more for the sprinters 169.9 kilometers a fourth category climb near the end a Tailwind as well and the intermediate spin spat bang in the middle a warm day confirmed the ice packs have been on at the start 29 degrees at the start rising to well above 30 by the end it looked as though we're going to have a big battle for the Breakaway full ride has got away they look set and then the radio communication started Oliveira would drop back as would abrahamson and then broke her door as well leaving Paul Simon gulielmi to ask if he was meant to stay up the road the answer was yes and he soon got a big gap as today pogacho the stage previous star and the new yellow Jersey rolled along for an easier start the Gap was about seven and a half minutes when Lotto Destiny took things up they were ably assisted by a couple of other Sprint teams including alpacinda kernick who'd won the two student stages prior to the Pyrenees maximum 20 points then for guliani at the intermediate Sprint Peloton by this stage though had almost brought it back Binion Jeremiah looked good it was between him and Phillips up took the 17 points Philips a content with the 15 and an extension to his lead in the green jersey competition and then the fireworks started in the white jersey and brown shorts and in the blue shorts pierlator making it across to goliel meat half a minute the Gap with 75ks to go and then they try their best to build me naturally was the first to lose contact and with six and a half kilometers to go the Breakaway Duo was still out in front and what fanart was deciding that this wasn't a Sprint for him lateral the final Rider to be caught three and a half case to go and the Riders already by this point in the zone where any crashes or incidents would mean that no time was lost no time lost in setting up the Sprint inside the final kilometer Honda pool starting his leader at around 600 meters to go Phillips and following moved up in the blue on the left-hand side and mass Peterson was always in it and only at this point did Mark Cavendish come into it he saw a gap thunderpool stopped the lead out maybe a little early Cavendish hit the front and neutral fans around the world were dreaming of 35 the record had to be broken or was it as Jasper to philipsa with a perfectly timed Sprint made it a hat-trick utterly dominant Cavendish having to settle for second is best placing in any Sprint yet same for opinion who was third not SATA with a good fourth place and grunavagan not getting going at this tour in fifth place cavity is seeing his opportunity and going a winner involved all 13 years previous but Jasper Phillips are the man at the moment the fastest Sprinter at this tour if you add up the stages at the end of the last tour five in succession now Philips are on fire three stage wins and the AIM now surely has to be to cement the green jersey a third of the way through this tour and Eunice is the leader it's not all cut and dry it seems a two-horse race at the very top but for the podium it is open General classification with an interesting day on the horizon sees vinegar 25 seconds ahead of pogachar Finley at 134 in third and then it's 314 from first to Simon yet Limoges a probable Sprint but with much more climbing and lumpy terrain this time many more carrots for the Breakaway and an interesting one in the finale a downhill then an uphill in the final kilometer Limoges the scene of a hotly contested Sprint a few years back when Marcel Kittle only just beat Brian cooker you can watch it live and on demand as always on the home of cycling on GCM Plus thank you
